text.title: Feature Discovery Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Feature Discovery application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Each candidate MUST carry RICE values (Reach / Impact / Confidence / Effort) with numeric inputs and a unit. 'Gut score' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Numeric inputs survive review; gut scores get re-litigated.

rule.statement: Promoting a feature to the top-5 requires ≥3 distinct evidence sources (interviews / tickets / behavioural signals). Single-customer requests are parked.
rule.rationale: Single-customer requests are anecdotal and inflate the roadmap with low-impact work.

rule.statement: Each top-5 feature names a single owner (handle + role). 'Team' or any placeholder value is rejected.
rule.rationale: Ownerless top features stall; named owners ship.

rule.statement: Each parked feature carries a revisit trigger (measurable threshold or dated event). Open-ended 'maybe later' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without revisit triggers the board accumulates dead candidates that crowd the live ones.

rule.statement: Top-5 total Effort MUST fit the next planning cycle's capacity. Overflow forces the lowest-RICE entry to drop or split.
rule.rationale: Over-committed roadmaps lie; capacity-bound top-5 ships.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
